children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation shall insure the deposits of all insured depository institutions as provided in this chapter.

children.heading: Net amount of insured deposit
children.label: B Net amount of insured deposit
children.text: The net amount due to any depositor at an insured depository institution shall not exceed the standard maximum deposit insurance amount as determined in accordance with subparagraphs (C), (D), (E) and (F) and paragraph (3).

children.heading: Aggregation of deposits
children.label: C Aggregation of deposits
children.text: For the purpose of determining the net amount due to any depositor under subparagraph (B), the Corporation shall aggregate the amounts of all deposits in the insured depository institution which are maintained by a depositor in the same capacity and the same right for the benefit of the depositor either in the name of the depositor or in the name of any other person, other than any amount in a trust fund described in paragraph (1) or (2) of or any funds described in .section 1817(i) of this titlesection 1817(i)(3) of this title

children.heading: Coverage for certain employee benefit plan deposits
children.label: D Coverage for certain employee benefit plan deposits

children.heading: Pass-through insurance
children.label: i Pass-through insurance
children.text: The Corporation shall provide pass-through deposit insurance for the deposits of any employee benefit plan.

children.heading: Prohibition on acceptance of benefit plan deposits
children.label: ii Prohibition on acceptance of benefit plan deposits
children.text: An insured depository institution that is not well capitalized or adequately capitalized may not accept employee benefit plan deposits.

children.heading: Capital standards
children.label: I Capital standards
children.text: The terms “well capitalized” and “adequately capitalized” have the same meanings as in section 1831 of this title.o

children.heading: Employee benefit plan
children.label: II Employee benefit plan
children.text: The term “employee benefit plan” has the same meaning as in paragraph (5)(B)(ii), and includes any eligible deferred compensation plan described in .section 457 of title 26

children.heading: Pass-through deposit insurance
children.label: III Pass-through deposit insurance
children.text: The term “pass-through deposit insurance” means, with respect to an employee benefit plan, deposit insurance coverage based on the interest of each participant, in accordance with regulations issued by the Corporation.

children.heading: Standard maximum deposit insurance amount defined
children.label: E Standard maximum deposit insurance amount defined
children.text: For purposes of this chapter, the term “standard maximum deposit insurance amount” means $250,000, adjusted as provided under subparagraph (F) after . Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the increase in the standard maximum deposit insurance amount to $250,000 shall apply to depositors in any institution for which the Corporation was appointed as receiver or conservator on or after , and before . The Corporation shall take such actions as are necessary to carry out the requirements of this section with respect to such depositors, without regard to any time limitations under this chapter. In implementing this and the preceding 2 sentences, any payment on a deposit claim made by the Corporation as receiver or conservator to a depositor above the standard maximum deposit insurance amount in effect at the time of the appointment of the Corporation as receiver or conservator shall be deemed to be part of the net amount due to the depositor under subparagraph (B).March 31, 2010January 1, 2008October 3, 2008

children.label: II
children.text: the ratio of the published annual value of the Personal Consumption Expenditures Chain-Type Price Index (or any successor index thereto), published by the Department of Commerce, for the calendar year preceding the year in which the adjustment is calculated under this clause, to the published annual value of such index for the calendar year preceding .April 1, 2006

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: ii Rounding
children.text: If the amount determined under clause (ii) for any period is not a multiple of $10,000, the amount so determined shall be rounded down to the nearest $10,000.

children.heading: Publication and report to the Congress
children.label: iii Publication and report to the Congress
I
children.text: publish in the Federal Register the standard maximum deposit insurance amount, the standard maximum share insurance amount, and the amount of coverage under paragraph (3)(A) and , as so calculated; andsection 1787(k)(3) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: jointly submit a report to the Congress containing the amounts described in subclause (I).

children.heading: 6-month implementation period
children.label: iv 6-month implementation period
children.text: Unless an Act of Congress enacted before July 1 of the calendar year in which an adjustment is required to be calculated under clause (i) provides otherwise, the increase in the standard maximum deposit insurance amount and the standard maximum share insurance amount shall take effect on January 1 of the year immediately succeeding such calendar year.

children.label: II

children.text: whether the increase will cause the reserve ratio of the fund to fall below 1.15 percent of estimated insured deposits.

children.heading: Authority to limit deposits
children.label: C Authority to limit deposits
children.text: The Corporation may limit the aggregate amount of funds that may be invested or deposited in deposits in any insured depository institution by any government depositor on the basis of the size of any such bank in terms of its assets: , such limitation may be exceeded by the pledging of acceptable securities to the government depositor when and where required.1So in original. children.heading: Amounts taken into account
children.label: B Amounts taken into account
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the amount aggregated for insurance coverage under this paragraph shall consist of the present vested and ascertainable interest of each participant under the plan, excluding any remainder interest created by, or as a result of, the plan.

children.heading: Uses
children.label: B Uses
children.text: The Deposit Insurance Fund shall be available to the Corporation for use with respect to insured depository institutions the deposits of which are insured by the Deposit Insurance Fund.

children.label: i
children.text: any insured depository institution for which the Corporation has been appointed conservator or receiver, in connection with any type of resolution by the Corporation;

children.label: ii
children.text: any other insured depository institution in default or in danger of default, in connection with any type of resolution by the Corporation; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any insured depository institution, in connection with the provision of assistance under this section or with respect to such institution, except that this clause shall not prohibit any assistance to any insured depository institution that is not in default, or that is not in danger of default, that is acquiring (as defined in ) another insured depository institution.section 1823 of this titlesection 1823(f)(8)(B) of this title

children.heading: Deposits
children.label: D Deposits
children.text: All amounts assessed against insured depository institutions by the Corporation shall be deposited into the Deposit Insurance Fund.

children.heading: Certain investment contracts not treated as insured deposits
children.label: 5 Certain investment contracts not treated as insured deposits

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A liability of an insured depository institution shall not be treated as an insured deposit if the liability arises under any insured depository institution investment contract between any insured depository institution and any employee benefit plan which expressly permits benefit-responsive withdrawals or transfers.

children.heading: Benefit-responsive withdrawals or transfers
children.label: i Benefit-responsive withdrawals or transfers
children.text: The term “benefit-responsive withdrawals or transfers” means any withdrawal or transfer of funds (consisting of any portion of the principal and any interest credited at a rate guaranteed by the insured depository institution investment contract) during the period in which any guaranteed rate is in effect, without substantial penalty or adjustment, to pay benefits provided by the employee benefit plan or to permit a plan participant or beneficiary to redirect the investment of his or her account balance.

children.label: I
children.text: has the meaning given to such term in ; andsection 1002(3) of title 29

children.label: II
children.text: includes any plan described in .section 401(d) of title 26

children.heading: Liquidation as closing of depository institution
children.label: b Liquidation as closing of depository institution
children.text: For the purposes of this chapter an insured depository institution shall be deemed to have been closed on account of inability to meet the demands of its depositors in any case in which it has been closed for the purpose of liquidation without adequate provision being made for payment of its depositors.

children.heading: Appointment of Corporation as conservator or receiver
children.label: c Appointment of Corporation as conservator or receiver

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of Federal law, the law of any State, or the constitution of any State, the Corporation may accept appointment and act as conservator or receiver for any insured depository institution upon appointment in the manner provided in paragraph (2) or (3).

children.heading: Conservator
children.label: i Conservator
children.text: The Corporation may, at the discretion of the supervisory authority, be appointed conservator of any insured Federal depository institution and the Corporation may accept such appointment.

children.heading: Receiver
children.label: ii Receiver
children.text: The Corporation shall be appointed receiver, and shall accept such appointment, whenever a receiver is appointed for the purpose of liquidation or winding up the affairs of an insured Federal depository institution by the appropriate Federal banking agency, notwithstanding any other provision of Federal law.

children.heading: Additional powers
children.label: B Additional powers
children.text: In addition to and not in derogation of the powers conferred and the duties imposed by this section on the Corporation as conservator or receiver, the Corporation, to the extent not inconsistent with such powers and duties, shall have any other power conferred on or any duty (which is related to the exercise of such power) imposed on a conservator or receiver for any Federal depository institution under any other provision of law.

children.heading: Corporation not subject to any other agency
children.label: C Corporation not subject to any other agency
children.text: When acting as conservator or receiver pursuant to an appointment described in subparagraph (A), the Corporation shall not be subject to the direction or supervision of any other agency or department of the United States or any State in the exercise of the Corporation’s rights, powers, and privileges.

children.heading: Depository institution in conservatorship subject to banking agency supervision
children.label: D Depository institution in conservatorship subject to banking agency supervision
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (C), any Federal depository institution for which the Corporation has been appointed conservator shall remain subject to the supervision of the appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.heading: Appointment by appropriate State supervisor
children.label: A Appointment by appropriate State supervisor
children.text: Whenever the authority having supervision of any insured State depository institution appoints a conservator or receiver for such institution and tenders appointment to the Corporation, the Corporation may accept such appointment.

children.heading: Additional powers
children.label: B Additional powers
children.text: In addition to the powers conferred and the duties related to the exercise of such powers imposed by State law on any conservator or receiver appointed under the law of such State for an insured State depository institution, the Corporation, as conservator or receiver pursuant to an appointment described in subparagraph (A), shall have the powers conferred and the duties imposed by this section on the Corporation as conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Corporation not subject to any other agency
children.label: C Corporation not subject to any other agency
children.text: When acting as conservator or receiver pursuant to an appointment described in subparagraph (A), the Corporation shall not be subject to the direction or supervision of any other agency or department of the United States or any State in the exercise of its rights, powers, and privileges.

children.heading: Depository institution in conservatorship subject to banking agency supervision
children.label: D Depository institution in conservatorship subject to banking agency supervision
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (C), any insured State depository institution for which the Corporation has been appointed conservator shall remain subject to the supervision of the appropriate State bank or savings association supervisor.

children.label: 4 Appointment of Corporation by the Corporation

children.heading: Grounds for appointing conservator or receiver
children.label: 5 Grounds for appointing conservator or receiver

children.heading: .—Assets insufficient for obligations
children.label: A .—Assets insufficient for obligations
children.text: The institution’s assets are less than the institution’s obligations to its creditors and others, including members of the institution.

children.label: i
children.text: any violation of any statute or regulation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any unsafe or unsound practice.

children.heading: .—Unsafe or unsound condition
children.label: C .—Unsafe or unsound condition
children.text: An unsafe or unsound condition to transact business.

children.heading: .—Cease and desist orders
children.label: D .—Cease and desist orders
children.text: Any willful violation of a cease-and-desist order which has become final.

children.heading: .—Concealment
children.label: E .—Concealment
children.text: Any concealment of the institution’s books, papers, records, or assets, or any refusal to submit the institution’s books, papers, records, or affairs for inspection to any examiner or to any lawful agent of the appropriate Federal banking agency or State bank or savings association supervisor.

children.heading: .—Inability to meet obligations
children.label: F .—Inability to meet obligations
children.text: The institution is likely to be unable to pay its obligations or meet its depositors’ demands in the normal course of business.

children.heading: .—Losses
children.label: G .—Losses
children.text: The institution has incurred or is likely to incur losses that will deplete all or substantially all of its capital, and there is no reasonable prospect for the institution to become adequately capitalized (as defined in section 1831(b) of this title) without Federal assistance.o

children.label: i
children.text: cause insolvency or substantial dissipation of assets or earnings;

children.label: ii
children.text: weaken the institution’s condition; or

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise seriously prejudice the interests of the institution’s depositors or the Deposit Insurance Fund.

children.heading: .—Consent
children.label: I .—Consent
children.text: The institution, by resolution of its board of directors or its shareholders or members, consents to the appointment.

children.heading: .—Cessation of insured status
children.label: J .—Cessation of insured status
children.text: The institution ceases to be an insured institution.

children.label: i
children.text: has no reasonable prospect of becoming adequately capitalized (as defined in that section);

children.label: ii
children.text: fails to become adequately capitalized when required to do so under section 1831(f)(2)(A) of this title;o

children.label: iii
children.text: fails to submit a capital restoration plan acceptable to that agency within the time prescribed under section 1831(e)(2)(D) of this title; oro

children.label: iv
children.text: materially fails to implement a capital restoration plan submitted and accepted under section 1831(e)(2) of this title.o

children.label: i
children.text: is critically undercapitalized, as defined in section 1831(b) of this title; oro

children.label: ii
children.text: otherwise has substantially insufficient capital.

children.heading: .—Money laundering offense
children.label: M .—Money laundering offense
children.text: The Attorney General notifies the appropriate Federal banking agency or the Corporation in writing that the insured depository institution has been found guilty of a criminal offense under section 1956 or 1957 of title 18 or section 5322 or 5324 of title 31.

children.heading: Appointment by Comptroller of the Currency
children.label: 6 Appointment by Comptroller of the Currency

children.heading: Conservator
children.label: A Conservator
children.text: The Corporation may, at the discretion of the Comptroller of the Currency, be appointed conservator and the Corporation may accept any such appointment.

children.heading: Receiver
children.label: B Receiver
children.text: The Corporation may, at the discretion of the Comptroller of the Currency, be appointed receiver and the Corporation may accept any such appointment.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: 7 Judicial review
children.text: If the Corporation is appointed (including the appointment of the Corporation as receiver by the Board of Directors) as conservator or receiver of a depository institution under paragraph (4), (9), or (10), the depository institution may, not later than 30 days thereafter, bring an action in the United States district court for the judicial district in which the home office of such depository institution is located, or in the United States District Court for the District of Columbia, for an order requiring the Corporation to be removed as the conservator or receiver (regardless of how such appointment was made), and the court shall, upon the merits, dismiss such action or direct the Corporation to be removed as the conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Replacement of conservator of State depository institution
children.label: 8 Replacement of conservator of State depository institution

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any insured State depository institution for which the Corporation appointed itself as conservator pursuant to paragraph (4), the Corporation may, without any requirement of notice, hearing, or other action, replace itself as conservator with itself as receiver of such institution.

children.heading: Replacement treated as removal of incumbent
children.label: B Replacement treated as removal of incumbent
children.text: The replacement of a conservator with a receiver under subparagraph (A) shall be treated as the removal of the Corporation as conservator.

children.heading: Right of review of original appointment not affected
children.label: C Right of review of original appointment not affected
children.text: The replacement of a conservator with a receiver under subparagraph (A) shall not affect any right of the insured State depository institution to obtain review, pursuant to paragraph (7), of the original appointment of the conservator.

children.heading: Appropriate Federal banking agency may appoint Corporation as conservator or receiver for insured State depository institution to carry out section 1831o
children.label: 9 Appropriate Federal banking agency may appoint Corporation as conservator or receiver for insured State depository institution to carry out section 1831o
i
children.text: 1 or more of the grounds specified in subparagraphs (K) and (L) of paragraph (5) exist with respect to that institution; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the appointment is necessary to carry out the purpose of section 1831 of this title.o

children.heading: Nondelegation
children.label: B Nondelegation
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agency shall not delegate any action under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Corporation may appoint itself as conservator or receiver for insured depository institution to prevent loss to Deposit Insurance Fund
children.label: 10 Corporation may appoint itself as conservator or receiver for insured depository institution to prevent loss to Deposit Insurance Fund
A
children.text: 1 or more of the grounds specified in any subparagraph of paragraph (5) exist with respect to the institution; and

children.label: i
children.text: the risk that the Deposit Insurance Fund would incur a loss with respect to the insured depository institution, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any loss that the Deposit Insurance Fund is expected to incur with respect to that institution.

children.heading: Appropriate Federal banking agency shall not appoint conservator under certain provisions without giving Corporation opportunity to appoint receiver
children.label: 11 Appropriate Federal banking agency shall not appoint conservator under certain provisions without giving Corporation opportunity to appoint receiver
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agency shall not appoint a conservator for an insured depository institution under subparagraph (K) or (L) of paragraph (5) without the Corporation’s consent unless the agency has given the Corporation 48 hours notice of the agency’s intention to appoint the conservator and the grounds for the appointment.

children.heading: Directors not liable for acquiescing in appointment of conservator or receiver
children.label: 12 Directors not liable for acquiescing in appointment of conservator or receiver
A
children.text: the appointment of the Corporation as conservator or receiver for that institution; or

children.label: B
children.text: an acquisition or combination under section 1831(f)(2)(A)(iii) of this title.o

children.label: A
children.text: this section shall apply to the Corporation as conservator or receiver in the same manner and to the same extent as if that institution were a Federal depository institution for which the Corporation had been appointed conservator or receiver; and

children.label: i
children.text: liquidate the institution in an orderly manner; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make any other disposition of any matter concerning the institution, as the Corporation determines is in the best interests of the institution, the depositors of the institution, and the Corporation.

children.heading: Powers and duties of Corporation as conservator or receiver
children.label: d Powers and duties of Corporation as conservator or receiver

children.heading: Rulemaking authority of Corporation
children.label: 1 Rulemaking authority of Corporation
children.text: The Corporation may prescribe such regulations as the Corporation determines to be appropriate regarding the conduct of conservatorships or receiverships.

children.label: i
children.text: all rights, titles, powers, and privileges of the insured depository institution, and of any stockholder, member, accountholder, depositor, officer, or director of such institution with respect to the institution and the assets of the institution; and

children.label: ii
children.text: title to the books, records, and assets of any previous conservator or other legal custodian of such institution.

children.label: i
children.text: take over the assets of and operate the insured depository institution with all the powers of the members or shareholders, the directors, and the officers of the institution and conduct all business of the institution;

children.label: ii
children.text: collect all obligations and money due the institution;

children.label: iii
children.text: perform all functions of the institution in the name of the institution which are consistent with the appointment as conservator or receiver; and

children.label: iv
children.text: preserve and conserve the assets and property of such institution.

children.heading: Functions of institution’s officers, directors, and shareholders
children.label: C Functions of institution’s officers, directors, and shareholders
children.text: The Corporation may, by regulation or order, provide for the exercise of any function by any member or stockholder, director, or officer of any insured depository institution for which the Corporation has been appointed conservator or receiver.

children.label: i
children.text: necessary to put the insured depository institution in a sound and solvent condition; and

children.label: ii
children.text: appropriate to carry on the business of the institution and preserve and conserve the assets and property of the institution.

children.heading: Additional powers as receiver
children.label: E Additional powers as receiver
children.text: The Corporation may (subject to the provisions of ), as receiver, place the insured depository institution in liquidation and proceed to realize upon the assets of the institution, having due regard to the conditions of credit in the locality.section 1831q of this title

children.heading: Organization of new institutions
children.label: F Organization of new institutions
children.text: The Corporation may, as receiver, with respect to any insured depository institution, organize a new depository institution under subsection (m) or a bridge depository institution under subsection (n).

children.heading: Merger; transfer of assets and liabilities
children.label: G Merger; transfer of assets and liabilities
I
children.text: merge the insured depository institution with another insured depository institution; or

children.label: II
children.text: subject to clause (ii), transfer any asset or liability of the institution in default (including assets and liabilities associated with any trust business) without any approval, assignment, or consent with respect to such transfer.

children.heading: Approval by appropriate Federal banking agency
children.label: ii Approval by appropriate Federal banking agency
children.text: No transfer described in clause (i)(II) may be made to another depository institution (other than a new depository institution or a bridge depository institution established pursuant to subsection (m) or (n)) without the approval of the appropriate Federal banking agency for such institution.

children.heading: Payment of valid obligations
children.label: H Payment of valid obligations
children.text: The Corporation, as conservator or receiver, shall pay all valid obligations of the insured depository institution in accordance with the prescriptions and limitations of this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Corporation may, as conservator, receiver, or exclusive manager and for purposes of carrying out any power, authority, or duty with respect to an insured depository institution (including determining any claim against the institution and determining and realizing upon any asset of any person in the course of collecting money due the institution), exercise any power established under , and the provisions of such section shall apply with respect to the exercise of any such power under this subparagraph in the same manner as such provisions apply under such section.section 1818(n) of this title

children.heading: Authority of Board of Directors
children.label: ii Authority of Board of Directors
children.text: A subpoena or subpoena duces tecum may be issued under clause (i) only by, or with the written approval of, the Board of Directors or their designees (or, in the case of a subpoena or subpoena duces tecum issued by the Resolution Trust Corporation under this subparagraph and section 1441a(b)(4) of this title, only by, or with the written approval of, the Board of Directors of such Corporation or their designees).2See References in Text note below.2

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: iii Rule of construction
children.text: This subsection shall not be construed as limiting any rights that the Corporation, in any capacity, might otherwise have under .section 1820(c) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: exercise all powers and authorities specifically granted to conservators or receivers, respectively, under this chapter and such incidental powers as shall be necessary to carry out such powers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: take any action authorized by this chapter,

children.heading: Utilization of private sector
children.label: K Utilization of private sector
children.text: In carrying out its responsibilities in the management and disposition of assets from insured depository institutions, as conservator, receiver, or in its corporate capacity, the Corporation shall utilize the services of private persons, including real estate and loan portfolio asset management, property management, auction marketing, legal, and brokerage services, only if such services are available in the private sector and the Corporation determines utilization of such services is the most practicable, efficient, and cost effective.

children.heading: Authority of receiver to determine claims
children.label: 3 Authority of receiver to determine claims

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation may, as receiver, determine claims in accordance with the requirements of this subsection and regulations prescribed under paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: promptly publish a notice to the depository institution’s creditors to present their claims, together with proof, to the receiver by a date specified in the notice which shall be not less than 90 days after the publication of such notice; and

children.label: ii
children.text: republish such notice approximately 1 month and 2 months, respectively, after the publication under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: at the creditor’s last address appearing in such books; or

children.label: ii
children.text: upon discovery of the name and address of a claimant not appearing on the institution’s books within 30 days after the discovery of such name and address.

children.heading: Rulemaking authority relating to determination of claims
children.label: 4 Rulemaking authority relating to determination of claims

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation may prescribe regulations regarding the allowance or disallowance of claims by the receiver and providing for administrative determination of claims and review of such determination.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the handling of receiverships of insured depository institutions, to maintain essential liquidity and to prevent financial disruption, the Corporation may, after the declaration of an institution’s insolvency, settle all uninsured and unsecured claims on the receivership with a final settlement payment which shall constitute full payment and disposition of the Corporation’s obligations to such claimants.

children.heading: Final settlement payment
children.label: ii Final settlement payment
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), a final settlement payment shall be payment of an amount equal to the product of the final settlement payment rate and the amount of the uninsured and unsecured claim on the receivership; and

children.heading: Final settlement payment rate
children.label: iii Final settlement payment rate
children.text: For purposes of clause (ii), the final settlement payment rate shall be a percentage rate reflecting an average of the Corporation’s receivership recovery experience, determined by the Corporation in such a way that over such time period as the Corporation may deem appropriate, the Corporation in total will receive no more or less than it would have received in total as a general creditor standing in the place of insured depositors in each specific receivership.

children.heading: Corporation authority
children.label: iv Corporation authority
children.text: The Corporation may undertake such supervisory actions and promulgate such regulations as may be necessary to assure that the requirements of this section can be implemented with respect to each insured depository institution in the event of its insolvency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Before the end of the 180-day period beginning on the date any claim against a depository institution is filed with the Corporation as receiver, the Corporation shall determine whether to allow or disallow the claim and shall notify the claimant of any determination with respect to such claim.

children.heading: Extension of time
children.label: ii Extension of time
children.text: The period described in clause (i) may be extended by a written agreement between the claimant and the Corporation.

children.label: I
children.text: on the depository institution’s books;

children.label: II
children.text: in the claim filed by the claimant; or

children.label: III
children.text: in documents submitted in proof of the claim.

children.heading: Contents of notice of disallowance
children.label: iv Contents of notice of disallowance
I
children.text: a statement of each reason for the disallowance; and

children.label: II
children.text: the procedures available for obtaining agency review of the determination to disallow the claim or judicial determination of the claim.

children.heading: Allowance of proven claims
children.label: B Allowance of proven claims
children.text: The receiver shall allow any claim received on or before the date specified in the notice published under paragraph (3)(B)(i) by the receiver from any claimant which is proved to the satisfaction of the receiver.

children.heading: Disallowance of claims filed after end of filing period
children.label: C Disallowance of claims filed after end of filing period

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), claims filed after the date specified in the notice published under paragraph (3)(B)(i) shall be disallowed and such disallowance shall be final.

children.label: I
children.text: the claimant did not receive notice of the appointment of the receiver in time to file such claim before such date; and

children.label: II
children.text: such claim is filed in time to permit payment of such claim.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The receiver may disallow any portion of any claim by a creditor or claim of security, preference, or priority which is not proved to the satisfaction of the receiver.

children.heading: Payments to less than fully secured creditors
children.label: ii Payments to less than fully secured creditors
I
children.text: may treat the portion of such claim which exceeds an amount equal to the fair market value of such property or other asset as an unsecured claim against the institution; and

children.label: II
children.text: may not make any payment with respect to such unsecured portion of the claim other than in connection with the disposition of all claims of unsecured creditors of the institution.

children.label: I
children.text: any extension of credit from any Federal home loan bank or Federal Reserve bank to any insured depository institution; or

children.label: II
children.text: any security interest in the assets of the institution securing any such extension of credit.

children.heading: No judicial review of determination pursuant to subparagraph (D)
children.label: E No judicial review of determination pursuant to subparagraph (D)
children.text: No court may review the Corporation’s determination pursuant to subparagraph (D) to disallow a claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitation tolled
children.label: i Statute of limitation tolled
children.text: For purposes of any applicable statute of limitations, the filing of a claim with the receiver shall constitute a commencement of an action.

children.heading: No prejudice to other actions
children.label: ii No prejudice to other actions
children.text: Subject to paragraph (12), the filing of a claim with the receiver shall not prejudice any right of the claimant to continue any action which was filed before the appointment of the receiver.

children.heading: Provision for agency review or judicial determination of claims
children.label: 6 Provision for agency review or judicial determination of claims
i
children.text: the end of the period described in paragraph (5)(A)(i) with respect to any claim against a depository institution for which the Corporation is receiver; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of any notice of disallowance of such claim pursuant to paragraph (5)(A)(i),

children.label: i
children.text: request administrative review of any claim in accordance with subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (7); or

children.label: ii
children.text: file suit on such claim (or continue an action commenced before the appointment of the receiver),

children.heading: Administrative hearing
children.label: A Administrative hearing
children.text: If any claimant requests review under this subparagraph in lieu of filing or continuing any action under paragraph (6) and the Corporation agrees to such request, the Corporation shall consider the claim after opportunity for a hearing on the record. The final determination of the Corporation with respect to such claim shall be subject to judicial review under chapter 7 of title 5.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Corporation shall also establish such alternative dispute resolution processes as may be appropriate for the resolution of claims filed under paragraph (5)(A)(i).

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: ii Criteria
children.text: In establishing alternative dispute resolution processes, the Corporation shall strive for procedures which are expeditious, fair, independent, and low cost.

children.heading: Voluntary binding or nonbinding procedures
children.label: iii Voluntary binding or nonbinding procedures
children.text: The Corporation may establish both binding and nonbinding processes, which may be conducted by any government or private party, but all parties, including the claimant and the Corporation, must agree to the use of the process in a particular case.

children.heading: Consideration of incentives
children.label: iv Consideration of incentives
children.text: The Corporation shall seek to develop incentives for claimants to participate in the alternative dispute resolution process.

children.label: i
children.text: allege the existence of legally valid and enforceable or perfected security interests in assets of any depository institution for which the Corporation has been appointed receiver; and

children.label: ii
children.text: allege that irreparable injury will occur if the routine claims procedure is followed.

children.label: I
children.text: whether to allow or disallow such claim; or

children.label: II
children.text: whether such claim should be determined pursuant to the procedures established pursuant to paragraph (5); and

children.label: ii
children.text: notify the claimant of the determination, and if the claim is disallowed, provide a statement of each reason for the disallowance and the procedure for obtaining agency review or judicial determination.

children.heading: Period for filing or renewing suit
children.label: C Period for filing or renewing suit
i
children.text: the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date of the filing of a request for expedited relief; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date the Corporation denies the claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: D Statute of limitations
children.text: If an action described in subparagraph (C) is not filed, or the motion to renew a previously filed suit is not made, before the end of the 30-day period beginning on the date on which such action or motion may be filed in accordance with subparagraph (B), the claim shall be deemed to be disallowed as of the end of such period (other than any portion of such claim which was allowed by the receiver), such disallowance shall be final, and the claimant shall have no further rights or remedies with respect to such claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitation tolled
children.label: i Statute of limitation tolled
children.text: For purposes of any applicable statute of limitations, the filing of a claim with the receiver shall constitute a commencement of an action.

children.heading: No prejudice to other actions
children.label: ii No prejudice to other actions
children.text: Subject to paragraph (12), the filing of a claim with the receiver shall not prejudice any right of the claimant to continue any action which was filed before the appointment of the receiver.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: A Requirements
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), any agreement which does not meet the requirements set forth in shall not form the basis of, or substantially comprise, a claim against the receiver or the Corporation.section 1823(e) of this title

children.heading: Exception to contemporaneous execution requirement
children.label: B Exception to contemporaneous execution requirement
children.text: Notwithstanding section 1823(e)(2) of this title, any agreement relating to an extension of credit between a Federal home loan bank or Federal Reserve bank and any insured depository institution which was executed before the extension of credit by such bank to such institution shall be treated as having been executed contemporaneously with such extension of credit for purposes of subparagraph (A).2

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The receiver may, in the receiver’s discretion and to the extent funds are available, pay creditor claims which are allowed by the receiver, approved by the Corporation pursuant to a final determination pursuant to paragraph (7) or (8), or determined by the final judgment of any court of competent jurisdiction in such manner and amounts as are authorized under this chapter.

children.heading: Payment of dividends on claims
children.label: B Payment of dividends on claims
children.text: The receiver may, in the receiver’s sole discretion, pay dividends on proved claims at any time, and no liability shall attach to the Corporation (in such Corporation’s corporate capacity or as receiver), by reason of any such payment, for failure to pay dividends to a claimant whose claim is not proved at the time of any such payment.

children.heading: Rulemaking authority of Corporation
children.label: C Rulemaking authority of Corporation
children.text: The Corporation may prescribe such rules, including definitions of terms, as it deems appropriate to establish a single uniform interest rate for or to make payments of post insolvency interest to creditors holding proven claims against the receivership estates of insured Federal or State depository institutions following satisfaction by the receiver of the principal amount of all creditor claims.

children.label: i
children.text: Administrative expenses of the receiver.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any deposit liability of the institution.

children.label: iii
children.text: Any other general or senior liability of the institution (which is not a liability described in clause (iv) or (v)).

children.label: iv
children.text: Any obligation subordinated to depositors or general creditors (which is not an obligation described in clause (v)).

children.label: v
children.text: Any obligation to shareholders or members arising as a result of their status as shareholders or members (including any depository institution holding company or any shareholder or creditor of such company).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The provisions of subparagraph (A) shall not supersede the law of any State except to the extent such law is inconsistent with the provisions of such subparagraph, and then only to the extent of the inconsistency.

children.heading: Procedure for determination of inconsistency
children.label: ii Procedure for determination of inconsistency
children.text: Upon the Corporation’s own motion or upon the request of any person with a claim described in subparagraph (A) or any State which is submitted to the Corporation in accordance with procedures which the Corporation shall prescribe, the Corporation shall determine whether any provision of the law of any State is inconsistent with any provision of subparagraph (A) and the extent of any such inconsistency.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: iii Judicial review
children.text: The final determination of the Corporation under clause (ii) shall be subject to judicial review under chapter 7 of title 5.

children.heading: Accounting report
children.label: C Accounting report
children.text: Any distribution by the Corporation in connection with any claim described in subparagraph (A)(v) shall be accompanied by the accounting report required under paragraph (15)(B).

children.label: i
children.text: 45 days, in the case of any conservator; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 90 days, in the case of any receiver,

children.heading: Grant of stay by all courts required
children.label: B Grant of stay by all courts required
children.text: Upon receipt of a request by any conservator or receiver pursuant to subparagraph (A) for a stay of any judicial action or proceeding in any court with jurisdiction of such action or proceeding, the court shall grant such stay as to all parties.

children.heading: Prior final adjudication
children.label: A Prior final adjudication
children.text: The Corporation shall abide by any final unappealable judgment of any court of competent jurisdiction which was rendered before the appointment of the Corporation as conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Rights and remedies of conservator or receiver
children.label: B Rights and remedies of conservator or receiver
i
children.text: have all the rights and remedies available to the insured depository institution (before the appointment of such conservator or receiver) and the Corporation in its corporate capacity, including removal to Federal court and all appellate rights; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not be required to post any bond in order to pursue such remedies.

children.heading: No attachment or execution
children.label: C No attachment or execution
children.text: No attachment or execution may issue by any court upon assets in the possession of the receiver.

children.label: i
children.text: any claim or action for payment from, or any action seeking a determination of rights with respect to, the assets of any depository institution for which the Corporation has been appointed receiver, including assets which the Corporation may acquire from itself as such receiver; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any claim relating to any act or omission of such institution or the Corporation as receiver.

children.label: i
children.text: maximizes the net present value return from the sale or disposition of such assets;

children.label: ii
children.text: minimizes the amount of any loss realized in the resolution of cases;

children.label: iii
children.text: ensures adequate competition and fair and consistent treatment of offerors;

children.label: iv
children.text: prohibits discrimination on the basis of race, sex, or ethnic groups in the solicitation and consideration of offers; and

children.label: v
children.text: maximizes the preservation of the availability and affordability of residential real property for low- and moderate-income individuals.

children.heading: Statute of limitations for actions brought by conservator or receiver
children.label: 14 Statute of limitations for actions brought by conservator or receiver
I
children.text: the 6-year period beginning on the date the claim accrues; or

children.label: II
children.text: the period applicable under State law; and

children.label: I
children.text: the 3-year period beginning on the date the claim accrues; or

children.label: II
children.text: the period applicable under State law.

children.heading: Determination of the date on which a claim accrues
children.label: B Determination of the date on which a claim accrues
i
children.text: the date of the appointment of the Corporation as conservator or receiver; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the cause of action accrues.

children.heading: Revival of expired State causes of action
children.label: C Revival of expired State causes of action

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of any tort claim described in clause (ii) for which the statute of limitation applicable under State law with respect to such claim has expired not more than 5 years before the appointment of the Corporation as conservator or receiver, the Corporation may bring an action as conservator or receiver on such claim without regard to the expiration of the statute of limitation applicable under State law.

children.heading: Claims described
children.label: ii Claims described
children.text: A tort claim referred to in clause (i) is a claim arising from fraud, intentional misconduct resulting in unjust enrichment, or intentional misconduct resulting in substantial loss to the institution.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation as conservator or receiver shall, consistent with the accounting and reporting practices and procedures established by the Corporation, maintain a full accounting of each conservatorship and receivership or other disposition of institutions in default.

children.heading: Annual accounting or report
children.label: B Annual accounting or report
children.text: With respect to each conservatorship or receivership to which the Corporation was appointed, the Corporation shall make an annual accounting or report, as appropriate, available to the Secretary of the Treasury, the Comptroller General of the United States, and the authority which appointed the Corporation as conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Availability of reports
children.label: C Availability of reports
children.text: Any report prepared pursuant to subparagraph (B) shall be made available by the Corporation upon request to any shareholder of the depository institution for which the Corporation was appointed conservator or receiver or any other member of the public.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), after the end of the 6-year period beginning on the date the Corporation is appointed as receiver of an insured depository institution, the Corporation may destroy any records of such institution which the Corporation, in the Corporation’s discretion, determines to be unnecessary unless directed not to do so by a court of competent jurisdiction or governmental agency, or prohibited by law.

children.heading: Old records
children.label: ii Old records
children.text: Notwithstanding clause (i), the Corporation may destroy records of an insured depository institution which are at least 10 years old as of the date on which the Corporation is appointed as the receiver of such depository institution in accordance with clause (i) at any time after such appointment is final, without regard to the 6-year period of limitation contained in clause (i).

children.heading: Contracts with State housing finance authorities
children.label: 16 Contracts with State housing finance authorities

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation may enter into contracts with any State housing finance authority for the sale of mortgage-related assets (as such terms are defined in ) of any depository institution in default (including assets and liabilities associated with any trust business), such contracts to be effective in accordance with their terms without any further approval, assignment, or consent with respect thereto.section 1441a–1 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the State housing finance authority’s ability to acquire and service current, delinquent, and defaulted mortgage related assets;

children.label: ii
children.text: the State housing finance authority’s ability to further national housing policies;

children.label: iii
children.text: the State housing finance authority’s sensitivity to the impact of the sale of mortgage related assets upon the State and local communities;

children.label: iv
children.text: the costs to the Federal Government associated with alternative ownership or disposition of the mortgage related assets;

children.label: v
children.text: the minimization of future guaranties which may be required of the Federal Government;

children.label: vi
children.text: the maximization of mortgage related asset values; and

children.label: vii
children.text: the utilization of institutions currently established in mortgage related asset market activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation, as conservator or receiver for any insured depository institution, and any conservator appointed by the Comptroller of the Currency may avoid a transfer of any interest of an institution-affiliated party, or any person who the Corporation or conservator determines is a debtor of the institution, in property, or any obligation incurred by such party or person, that was made within 5 years of the date on which the Corporation or conservator was appointed conservator or receiver if such party or person voluntarily or involuntarily made such transfer or incurred such liability with the intent to hinder, delay, or defraud the insured depository institution, the Corporation or other conservator, or any other appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.label: i
children.text: the initial transferee of such transfer or the institution-affiliated party or person for whose benefit such transfer was made; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any immediate or mediate transferee of any such initial transferee.

children.heading: Rights of transferee or obligee
children.label: C Rights of transferee or obligee
i
children.text: any transferee that takes for value, including satisfaction or securing of a present or antecedent debt, in good faith; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any immediate or mediate good faith transferee of such transferee.

children.heading: Rights under this paragraph
children.label: D Rights under this paragraph
children.text: The rights under this paragraph of the Corporation and any conservator described in subparagraph (A) shall be superior to any rights of a trustee or any other party (other than any party which is a Federal agency) under title 11.

children.heading: Attachment of assets and other injunctive relief
children.label: 18 Attachment of assets and other injunctive relief
A
children.text: the Corporation (in the Corporation’s capacity as conservator or receiver for any insured depository institution or in the Corporation’s corporate capacity with respect to any asset acquired or liability assumed by the Corporation under this section or section 1822 or 1823 of this title); or

children.label: B
children.text: any conservator appointed by the Comptroller of the Currency,

children.heading: Showing
children.label: A Showing
children.text: Rule 65 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure shall apply with respect to any proceeding under paragraph (18) without regard to the requirement of such rule that the applicant show that the injury, loss, or damage is irreparable and immediate.

children.heading: State proceeding
children.label: B State proceeding
children.text: If, in the case of any proceeding in a State court, the court determines that rules of civil procedure available under the laws of such State provide substantially similar protections to such party’s right to due process as Rule 65 (as modified with respect to such proceeding by subparagraph (A)), the relief sought by the Corporation or a conservator pursuant to paragraph (18) may be requested under the laws of such State.

children.heading: Treatment of claims arising from breach of contracts executed by the receiver or conservator
children.label: 20 Treatment of claims arising from breach of contracts executed by the receiver or conservator
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, any final and unappealable judgment for monetary damages entered against a receiver or conservator for an insured depository institution for the breach of an agreement executed or approved by such receiver or conservator after the date of its appointment shall be paid as an administrative expense of the receiver or conservator. Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to limit the power of a receiver or conservator to exercise any rights under contract or law, including to terminate, breach, cancel, or otherwise discontinue such agreement.

children.heading: Provisions relating to contracts entered into before appointment of conservator or receiver
children.label: e Provisions relating to contracts entered into before appointment of conservator or receiver
A
children.text: to which such institution is a party;

children.label: B

children.label: C
children.text: the disaffirmance or repudiation of which the conservator or receiver determines, in the conservator’s or receiver’s discretion, will promote the orderly administration of the institution’s affairs.

children.heading: Timing of repudiation
children.label: 2 Timing of repudiation
children.text: The conservator or receiver appointed for any insured depository institution in accordance with subsection (c) shall determine whether or not to exercise the rights of repudiation under this subsection within a reasonable period following such appointment.

children.heading: No liability for other damages
children.label: B No liability for other damages
i
children.text: punitive or exemplary damages;

children.label: ii
children.text: damages for lost profits or opportunity; or

children.label: iii
children.text: damages for pain and suffering.

children.heading: Measure of damages for repudiation of financial contracts
children.label: C Measure of damages for repudiation of financial contracts
i

children.heading: Leases under which the institution is the lessee
children.label: 4 Leases under which the institution is the lessee

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the conservator or receiver disaffirms or repudiates a lease under which the insured depository institution was the lessee, the conservator or receiver shall not be liable for any damages (other than damages determined pursuant to subparagraph (B)) for the disaffirmance or repudiation of such lease.

children.label: I

children.label: II
children.text: the disaffirmance or repudiation becomes effective,

children.label: ii
children.text: have no claim for damages under any acceleration clause or other penalty provision in the lease; and

children.label: iii
children.text: have a claim for any unpaid rent, subject to all appropriate offsets and defenses, due as of the date of the appointment which shall be paid in accordance with this subsection and subsection (i).

children.heading: Leases under which the institution is the lessor
children.label: 5 Leases under which the institution is the lessor
i
children.text: treat the lease as terminated by such repudiation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: remain in possession of the leasehold interest for the balance of the term of the lease unless the lessee defaults under the terms of the lease after the date of such repudiation.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to lessee remaining in possession
children.label: B Provisions applicable to lessee remaining in possession
I
children.text: shall continue to pay the contractual rent pursuant to the terms of the lease after the date of the repudiation of such lease;

children.label: II

children.label: ii
children.text: the conservator or receiver shall not be liable to the lessee for any damages arising after such date as a result of the repudiation other than the amount of any offset allowed under clause (i)(II).

children.heading: Contracts for the sale of real property
children.label: 6 Contracts for the sale of real property
i
children.text: treat the contract as terminated by such repudiation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: remain in possession of such real property.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to purchaser remaining in possession
children.label: B Provisions applicable to purchaser remaining in possession
I
children.text: shall continue to make all payments due under the contract after the date of the repudiation of the contract; and

children.label: II
children.text: may offset against any such payments any damages which accrue after such date due to the nonperformance (after such date) of any obligation of the depository institution under the contract; and

children.label: I
children.text: not be liable to the purchaser for any damages arising after such date as a result of the repudiation other than the amount of any offset allowed under clause (i)(II);

children.label: II

children.label: III
children.text: have no obligation under the contract other than the performance required under subclause (II).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No provision of this paragraph shall be construed as limiting the right of the conservator or receiver to assign the contract described in subparagraph (A) and sell the property subject to the contract and the provisions of this paragraph.

children.heading: No liability after assignment and sale
children.label: ii No liability after assignment and sale
children.text: If an assignment and sale described in clause (i) is consummated, the conservator or receiver shall have no further liability under the contract described in subparagraph (A) or with respect to the real property which was the subject of such contract.

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: deemed to have arisen as of the date the conservator or receiver was appointed.

children.heading: Services performed after appointment and prior to repudiation
children.label: B Services performed after appointment and prior to repudiation
i
children.text: the other party shall be paid under the terms of the contract for the services performed; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of such payment shall be treated as an administrative expense of the conservatorship or receivership.

children.heading: Acceptance of performance no bar to subsequent repudiation
children.label: C Acceptance of performance no bar to subsequent repudiation
children.text: The acceptance by any conservator or receiver of services referred to in subparagraph (B) in connection with a contract described in such subparagraph shall not affect the right of the conservator or receiver to repudiate such contract under this section at any time after such performance.

children.heading: Rights of parties to contracts
children.label: A Rights of parties to contracts
i
children.text: any right such person has to cause the termination, liquidation, or acceleration of any qualified financial contract with an insured depository institution which arises upon the appointment of the Corporation as receiver for such institution at any time after such appointment;

children.label: ii
children.text: any right under any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to one or more qualified financial contracts described in clause (i);3So in original. Probably should be followed by “or”.3

children.label: iii
children.text: any right to offset or net out any termination value, payment amount, or other transfer obligation arising under or in connection with 1 or more contracts and agreements described in clause (i), including any master agreement for such contracts or agreements.

children.heading: Applicability of other provisions
children.label: B Applicability of other provisions
children.text: Subsection (d)(12) shall apply in the case of any judicial action or proceeding brought against any receiver referred to in subparagraph (A), or the insured depository institution for which such receiver was appointed, by any party to a contract or agreement described in subparagraph (A)(i) with such institution.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (11), or any other Federal or State law relating to the avoidance of preferential or fraudulent transfers, the Corporation, whether acting as such or as conservator or receiver of an insured depository institution, may not avoid any transfer of money or other property in connection with any qualified financial contract with an insured depository institution.section 91 of this title

children.heading: Exception for certain transfers
children.label: ii Exception for certain transfers
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any transfer of money or other property in connection with any qualified financial contract with an insured depository institution if the Corporation determines that the transferee had actual intent to hinder, delay, or defraud such institution, the creditors of such institution, or any conservator or receiver appointed for such institution.

children.heading: Certain contracts and agreements defined
children.label: D Certain contracts and agreements defined

children.heading: Qualified financial contract
children.label: i Qualified financial contract
children.text: The term “qualified financial contract” means any securities contract, commodity contract, forward contract, repurchase agreement, swap agreement, and any similar agreement that the Corporation determines by regulation, resolution, or order to be a qualified financial contract for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: means a contract for the purchase, sale, or loan of a security, a certificate of deposit, a mortgage loan, any interest in a mortgage loan, a group or index of securities, certificates of deposit, or mortgage loans or interests therein (including any interest therein or based on the value thereof) or any option on any of the foregoing, including any option to purchase or sell any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option, and including any repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction on any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option (whether or not such repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction is a “repurchase agreement”, as defined in clause (v));

children.label: II
children.text: does not include any purchase, sale, or repurchase obligation under a participation in a commercial mortgage loan unless the Corporation determines by regulation, resolution, or order to include any such agreement within the meaning of such term;

children.label: III
children.text: means any option entered into on a national securities exchange relating to foreign currencies;

children.label: IV
children.text: means the guarantee (including by novation) by or to any securities clearing agency of any settlement of cash, securities, certificates of deposit, mortgage loans or interests therein, group or index of securities, certificates of deposit, or mortgage loans or interests therein (including any interest therein or based on the value thereof) or option on any of the foregoing, including any option to purchase or sell any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option (whether or not such settlement is in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in subclauses (I) through (XII) (other than subclause (II));4So in original. The semicolon probably should be preceded by an additional closing parenthesis.4

children.label: V
children.text: means any margin loan;

children.label: VI
children.text: means any extension of credit for the clearance or settlement of securities transactions;

children.label: VII
children.text: means any loan transaction coupled with a securities collar transaction, any prepaid securities forward transaction, or any total return swap transaction coupled with a securities sale transaction;

children.label: VIII
children.text: means any other agreement or transaction that is similar to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: IX
children.text: means any combination of the agreements or transactions referred to in this clause;

children.label: X
children.text: means any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: XI
children.text: means a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), (VIII), (IX), or (X), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a securities contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a securities contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), (VIII), (IX), or (X); and

children.label: XII
children.text: means any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause, including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause.

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to a futures commission merchant, a contract for the purchase or sale of a commodity for future delivery on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade;

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to a foreign futures commission merchant, a foreign future;

children.label: III
children.text: with respect to a leverage transaction merchant, a leverage transaction;

children.label: IV
children.text: with respect to a clearing organization, a contract for the purchase or sale of a commodity for future delivery on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade that is cleared by such clearing organization, or commodity option traded on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade that is cleared by such clearing organization;

children.label: V
children.text: with respect to a commodity options dealer, a commodity option;

children.label: VI
children.text: any other agreement or transaction that is similar to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: VII
children.text: any combination of the agreements or transactions referred to in this clause;

children.label: VIII
children.text: any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: IX
children.text: a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), or (VIII), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a commodity contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a commodity contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), or (VIII); or

children.label: X
children.text: any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause, including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause.

children.label: I
children.text: a contract (other than a commodity contract) for the purchase, sale, or transfer of a commodity or any similar good, article, service, right, or interest which is presently or in the future becomes the subject of dealing in the forward contract trade, or product or byproduct thereof, with a maturity date more than 2 days after the date the contract is entered into, including, a repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction (whether or not such repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction is a “repurchase agreement”, as defined in clause (v)), consignment, lease, swap, hedge transaction, deposit, loan, option, allocated transaction, unallocated transaction, or any other similar agreement;5So in original. The comma probably should not appear.5

children.label: II
children.text: any combination of agreements or transactions referred to in subclauses (I) and (III);

children.label: III
children.text: any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I) or (II);

children.label: IV
children.text: a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclauses (I), (II), or (III), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a forward contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a forward contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (II), or (III); or

children.label: V
children.text: any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), or (IV), including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in any such subclause.

children.label: I
children.text: means an agreement, including related terms, which provides for the transfer of one or more certificates of deposit, mortgage-related securities (as such term is defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 [ et seq.]), mortgage loans, interests in mortgage-related securities or mortgage loans, eligible bankers’ acceptances, qualified foreign government securities or securities that are direct obligations of, or that are fully guaranteed by, the United States or any agency of the United States against the transfer of funds by the transferee of such certificates of deposit, eligible bankers’ acceptances, securities, mortgage loans, or interests with a simultaneous agreement by such transferee to transfer to the transferor thereof certificates of deposit, eligible bankers’ acceptances, securities, mortgage loans, or interests as described above, at a date certain not later than 1 year after such transfers or on demand, against the transfer of funds, or any other similar agreement;15 U.S.C. 78a

children.label: II
children.text: does not include any repurchase obligation under a participation in a commercial mortgage loan unless the Corporation determines by regulation, resolution, or order to include any such participation within the meaning of such term;

children.label: III
children.text: means any combination of agreements or transactions referred to in subclauses (I) and (IV);

children.label: IV
children.text: means any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I) or (III);

children.label: V
children.text: means a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (III), or (IV), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a repurchase agreement under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a repurchase agreement under this subclause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (III), or (IV); and

children.label: VI
children.text: means any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (III), (IV), or (V), including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in any such subclause.

children.label: I
children.text: any agreement, including the terms and conditions incorporated by reference in any such agreement, which is an interest rate swap, option, future, or forward agreement, including a rate floor, rate cap, rate collar, cross-currency rate swap, and basis swap; a spot, same day-tomorrow, tomorrow-next, forward, or other foreign exchange, precious metals, or other commodity agreement; a currency swap, option, future, or forward agreement; an equity index or equity swap, option, future, or forward agreement; a debt index or debt swap, option, future, or forward agreement; a total return, credit spread or credit swap, option, future, or forward agreement; a commodity index or commodity swap, option, future, or forward agreement; weather swap, option, future, or forward agreement; an emissions swap, option, future, or forward agreement; or an inflation swap, option, future, or forward agreement;

children.label: II
children.text: any agreement or transaction that is similar to any other agreement or transaction referred to in this clause and that is of a type that has been, is presently, or in the future becomes, the subject of recurrent dealings in the swap or other derivatives markets (including terms and conditions incorporated by reference in such agreement) and that is a forward, swap, future, option, or spot transaction on one or more rates, currencies, commodities, equity securities or other equity instruments, debt securities or other debt instruments, quantitative measures associated with an occurrence, extent of an occurrence, or contingency associated with a financial, commercial, or economic consequence, or economic or financial indices or measures of economic or financial risk or value;

children.label: III
children.text: any combination of agreements or transactions referred to in this clause;

children.label: IV
children.text: any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: V
